text.title: docker Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for docker as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using latest tag in production deployments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unpredictable deployments — pin specific versions with digest instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Security vulnerability — create non-root user (UID 1000+) instead.

failure-mode.description: Storing secrets in ENV or Dockerfile.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Visible in docker inspect and logs — use Docker secrets or vault instead.

failure-mode.description: Large monolithic images without multi-stage builds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Slow deploys and more vulnerabilities — use multi-stage builds and distroless base images.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No isolation — use custom networks with segmentation instead.

failure-mode.description: No health checks on production containers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent failures — add HEALTHCHECK to every production image.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding config values directly in images.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inflexible containers — use ENV variables and secrets instead.

failure-mode.description: Single RUN instruction per dependency installation line.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates many layers and large images — combine RUN commands with &&.
